scope: S4096 shared latent-KV sparse MLA backward compressed-topk route
trigger: compressed-only 11.02 ms accounts for about 76.5% of the full local+compressed raw backward slice

mechanism_read.not_route_index_load: The structured-index probe removes synthetic route tensor loads and saves only 0.03-0.06 ms at S4096.
mechanism_read.not_simple_hn_tile_choice: The compressed-only tile sweep is flat around H64/N64: best actual route-loading point is 10.997 ms, H64/N128 is 11.102 ms, H64/N256 is 11.322 ms, and smaller head tiles regress badly.
mechanism_read.not_wholesale_key_major_reduction: The two-pass inverse-route dKV reduction removes hot-path atomics but gives up query-major QK/probability reuse and writes large scratch; best S4096 point is 140.67 ms.
mechanism_read.not_naive_group_major_row_reduction: A compressed-only group-reduce diagnostic aggregates 512 token-head rows per compressed group and D block before one atomic-add. It passes S128 parity at 0.56-0.59 ms, but the real S4096/top-k512 shape is 333.86 ms. Lowering atomic count on paper is not enough if the traversal loops group-major over too many row blocks and loses query-major locality.
mechanism_read.dq_side_is_not_the_wall: The dQ-only recompute probe is 2.27 ms for the full 640-key local+compressed route.
mechanism_read.topk_scaling_is_near_linear: Compressed-only query-major latency scales with selected compressed groups: top-k 64/128/256/512 measures 2.01/3.61/6.34/10.95 ms. This rules out a large fixed launch/wrapper cost and keeps the wall tied to per-selected-group work.
mechanism_read.dkv_dot_math_is_not_the_wall: The diagnostic checksum-sink split keeps QK/probability/dQ work and computes dKV dot products, but replaces full tl.atomic_add dKV accumulation with one scalar store per tile. At top-k 512, no_dKV is 1.90 ms, checksum-sink is 2.68 ms, and full atomic is 10.89 ms; at top-k 256, no_dKV is 1.17 ms, checksum-sink is 1.65 ms, and full atomic is 6.38 ms. The dKV dot math is about 0.5-0.8 ms; full dKV atomic accumulation is about 4.7-8.2 ms.
mechanism_read.rocprof_confirms_kernel_wall: A retained-tile rocprof kernel trace isolates _sparse_mla_shared_kv_backward_dq_dkv_query_recompute_kernel at 11.06 ms avg for full atomic, 1.14 ms no-dKV, and 1.88 ms checksum-sink. The kernel-stat delta attributes about 0.74 ms to dKV dot math and about 9.18 ms to full compressed dKV atomic accumulation.
mechanism_read.rocprof_counters_confirm_atomic_volume: The H64/N64 counter sweep reports 16.78M atomic wavefronts and 402.52M TCC 32-byte atomic DRAM write requests in the full compressed dKV path. The no-dKV and checksum-sink controls both report zero atomic wavefronts and zero TCC atomic requests. The TCC request count is exactly half of the 805.04M scalar dKV atomic-add elements predicted by the route fan-in analyzer, so the selected compressed-group fan-in model matches hardware traffic. TCP_ATOMIC_TAGCONFLICT_STALL_CYCLES_sum is zero, so this counter set points to atomic traffic volume rather than a visible TCP tag-conflict stall counter.
mechanism_read.mfma_accumulation_floor_is_positive_but_: A compressed-prefix batched-GEMM floor turns the two dKV accumulation terms into KxS @ SxD bmm over heads. S128 FP32 formula parity passes with max diff 3.24e-5, and the mixed FP32-score/BF16-bmm contract passes with max diff 0.1298. At S4096, mixed mode is 7.07 ms and pure BF16 timing is 4.76 ms, versus about 11.0 ms for the retained query-major atomic path. This proves an MFMA-friendly compressed dKV topology can beat atomic accumulation, but the current full probe materializes [B,H,S,K] scores/probs and peaks at 4.3-5.9 GiB, so it is a design floor rather than a production backend.
mechanism_read.chunked_mfma_floor_bounds_materializatio: The chunked BMM floor keeps the mixed contract correct at S128 chunk32 with max diff 0.112. At S4096/top-k512, chunk2048 and chunk1024 retain the 7 ms band at 7.69-7.72 ms while reducing peak allocation to 4.51 and 3.88 GiB and active score/prob slabs to 0.25 and 0.125 GiB. Smaller external-BMM chunks bend upward: chunk512 is 8.11 ms and chunk256 is 9.51 ms. This says materialization can be bounded without returning to the 11 ms atomic wall, but a production path must fuse the streaming schedule rather than launch many separate BMM chunks.
mechanism_read.flat_gemm_floor_reduces_heads_directly: The flat-GEMM variant reshapes heads and tokens into HS rows and accumulates Kx(HS) @ (HS)xD directly, avoiding a separate head-local gradient output before reduction. It is viable but not a major mixed-mode speedup: full S4096 mixed is 7.05 ms and 5.60 GiB, essentially the 7.07 ms BMM floor with slightly lower memory. Chunk1024/chunk512 are 7.69/7.78 ms and 3.73/3.42 GiB, slightly better memory than BMM chunks. Pure BF16 flat-GEMM passes the S128 tolerance at max diff 0.198 and times S4096 at 4.73 ms with 4.10 GiB peak, making BF16 accumulation a plausible fused-kernel design point.
mechanism_read.compressed_topk_is_dense_enough_to_hurt: The compressed-only probe selects 512 compressed groups per query out of 1024 groups. The prefix-route fan-in analyzer reports 1,572,352 compressed selections at top-k 512, a 75.3% share of the full local+compressed route and about 805M estimated compressed dKV atomic-add elements at D=512/BLOCK_H=64.
mechanism_read.kernel_mechanism: The query-major kernel launches one program per token and head block, loops over selected keys in block_n chunks, recomputes QK and dO.V across four D=128 chunks, forms probabilities and grad logits, then reloads KV/Q/dO for each D chunk to compute dQ and dKV. dQ stays local, but dKV is tl.atomic_add back into the shared compressed KV gradient bank for every selected group and D chunk.
mechanism_read.likely_wall: The remaining wall is many-to-one full dKV atomic accumulation into the compressed shared-KV gradient bank under dynamic top-k routes. Repeated selected-KV reads and D-pass recompute still exist, but the profile split shows dKV dot math is small relative to the full atomic accumulation cost. It is not wrapper glue, index loading, a trivial Triton block retune, raw dense dot math, or a problem solved by a naive group-major row reduction.

completed_profile_cuts.name: topk_scaling_64_128_256_512
completed_profile_cuts.result: Compressed-only query-major latency rises 2.01 -> 3.61 -> 6.34 -> 10.95 ms as selected compressed top-k rises 64 -> 128 -> 256 -> 512.

completed_profile_cuts.name: dkv_no_atomic_or_throwaway_store
completed_profile_cuts.result: Diagnostic dkv_reduce_sink computes dKV dot products but avoids full atomic accumulation. At top-k512, full minus sink is about 8.21 ms while sink minus no_dKV is only about 0.79 ms; at top-k256, full minus sink is about 4.73 ms while sink minus no_dKV is about 0.48 ms.

completed_profile_cuts.name: compressed_dkv_group_major_row_reduce
completed_profile_cuts.result: The compressed-only group-reduce dKV probe passes formula parity, but S4096/top-k512 with block_rows=512 is 333.86 ms despite reducing the estimated atomic elements upper bound from about 805M to 134M. This rejects a naive all-groups/all-row-blocks reduction topology.

completed_profile_cuts.name: rocprof_retained_tile_kerneltrace
completed_profile_cuts.result: Rocprof kernel stats on the retained H64/D512 compressed-only path report the target backward kernel at 11.06 ms full atomic, 1.14 ms no-dKV, and 1.88 ms checksum-sink. This independently corroborates that the full compressed dKV atomic accumulation delta, about 9.18 ms, dominates the retained kernel.

completed_profile_cuts.name: rocprof_h64n64_atomic_counter_sweep
completed_profile_cuts.result: Hardware counters on the retained H64/N64 tile report 16.78M atomic wavefronts and 402.52M TCC 32-byte atomic DRAM write requests in the full path, versus zero atomic counters in no-dKV and checksum-sink controls. The TCC request count matches half of the 805.04M predicted scalar dKV atomic-add elements.

completed_profile_cuts.name: compressed_prefix_dkv_bmm_floor
completed_profile_cuts.result: A materializing batched-GEMM floor passes S128 in FP32 and mixed FP32-score/BF16-bmm modes, then times at 7.07 ms mixed and 4.76 ms pure BF16 for S4096/top-k512. This is a positive design floor for MFMA-friendly compressed dKV accumulation, not a production kernel because it materializes [B,H,S,K] scores/probs.

completed_profile_cuts.name: compressed_prefix_dkv_bmm_chunk_sweep
completed_profile_cuts.result: A chunked BMM floor passes S128 mixed chunk32 with max diff 0.112. At S4096/top-k512, chunk2048/chunk1024 keep median latency at 7.69/7.72 ms while lowering peak memory to 4.51/3.88 GiB; chunk512/chunk256 bend to 8.11/9.51 ms, exposing repeated external BMM launch and packing overhead.

completed_profile_cuts.name: compressed_prefix_dkv_flat_gemm_floor
completed_profile_cuts.result: Flattening heads and sequence into HS rows is viable but not a major mixed-mode speedup: S4096 mixed full is 7.05 ms and chunk1024/chunk512 are 7.69/7.78 ms. The useful improvement is memory and BF16 viability: chunk512 peak is 3.42 GiB, and pure BF16 passes S128 at max diff 0.198 while timing S4096 at 4.73 ms.

next_profile_cuts.name: dq_only_vs_dkv_only_compressed512
next_profile_cuts.purpose: Separate dQ math from dKV accumulation on the compressed-only route.
next_profile_cuts.expected_signal: If dQ remains around 1-2 ms and dKV consumes most of the 11 ms, prioritize dKV conflict/scatter scheduling.

next_profile_cuts.name: compressed_group_fan_in_histogram
next_profile_cuts.purpose: Count per-compressed-group selection fan-in over S4096 for actual LI top-k and synthetic prefix routes.
next_profile_cuts.expected_signal: Hot groups or high fan-in variance explain atomic contention and guide bucketed/conflict-aware reduction.

next_profile_cuts.name: dim_scaling_64_128_256_512
next_profile_cuts.purpose: Measure whether D=512 repeats are the bottleneck through four D=128 passes.
next_profile_cuts.expected_signal: Linear D scaling favors value/dKV math fusion; nonlinear scaling flags occupancy/register pressure or memory behavior.

next_profile_cuts.name: conflict_aware_compressed_dkv_microdesign
next_profile_cuts.purpose: Prototype a fused streaming/bucketed MFMA compressed dKV accumulation schedule that reduces atomic request volume without materializing [B,H,S,K] scores/probs or scanning all groups over all row blocks.
next_profile_cuts.expected_signal: Approach the 4.8-7.1 ms bmm floor while preserving the no-selected-state training contract and retaining S128 parity.

next_implementation_direction: Preserve the query-major FlashAttention-style reuse for QK/probability/dQ, but redesign compressed dKV accumulation around MFMA-friendly grouped KxS @ SxD work or an equivalent fused streaming/bucketed schedule. The next candidate should reduce atomic traffic without falling into the 140 ms key-major scratch-reduction trap, the 333 ms naive group-major row-reduction trap, the repeated-BMM overhead visible below chunk512, or the 4.3-5.9 GiB full materialized-probability footprint of the bmm floor, then fuse LI target-probability/KL derivative and compressor gradients into the same recompute path.
